step_by_step_thinking:
When Rb gene phosphorylation is defective, the RB protein remains hypophosphorylated and continues to bind and inhibit E2F transcription factors. This blocks the transition from G1 to S phase, effectively stopping the cell cycle at the G1 phase.
\boxed{A}
Your final answer must respect the above instructions and contain nothing else but the clinical reasoning and the selected option in boxed format.
When Rb gene phosphorylation is defective, the RB protein remains hypophosphorylated and actively binds to E2F transcription factors, preventing them from initiating genes necessary for entry into the S phase. This halts cell cycle progression specifically at the G1 phase, blocking passage into DNA replication.
